pi_items.source: Cross-referenced across setup_task.sh (line 29), export_result.sh (line 29), and task description - all confirm port 6661 for Token_Manager channel listening for JSON data
Cross-referenced across setup_task.sh (line 35), export_result.sh (line 30), and task description - all confirm port 6662 for Data_Sender channel listening for HL7 v2.x messages
Confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 32) which states 'Key: "current_bearer_token"' and task description which specifies storing the token 'under the key "current_bearer_token"'

Confirmed in multiple locations: setup_task.sh (line 9 creates the directory, line 38 documents the destination), export_result.sh (line 31 defines OUTPUT_DIR), and task description which specifies output path '/tmp/authenticated_output/msg_${message.messageId}.txt'

privileged_info_summary: The task implements a cross-channel token caching pattern in NextGen Connect integration engine. The Token_Manager channel listens on TCP port 6661 for JSON payloads containing access tokens, extracting the 'access_token' field and storing it in the Global Map under the key 'current_bearer_token'. The Data_Sender channel listens on TCP port 6662 for HL7 v2.x messages, retrieves the current bearer token from the Global Map, and writes output files to /tmp/authenticated_output/ with an 'Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>' header prepended to each HL7 message. All metadata values have been verified through code cross-referencing.
